questions.template: A fluid flows through a pipe with pressure {P1} Pa and velocity {v1} m/s at point 1. At point 2, the velocity increases to {v2} m/s. Both points are at the same height. What is the pressure at point 2? (Density of fluid = {rho} kg/m³)

Water (density = 1000 kg/m³) flows horizontally at {v1} m/s through a pipe at pressure {P1} Pa. The pipe narrows and the velocity increases to {v2} m/s. What is the pressure drop between the two points?
A fluid with density {rho} kg/m³ flows through a horizontal pipe. At point 1, pressure is {P1} Pa and velocity is {v1} m/s. At point 2, the pressure drops to {P2} Pa. What is the velocity at point 2?

Water (density = 1000 kg/m³) flows through a pipe. At point 1 (height {h1} m), pressure is {P1} Pa and velocity is {v1} m/s. At point 2 (height {h2} m), velocity is {v2} m/s. What is the pressure at point 2? (g = 9.8 m/s²)
